channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Linux filesystems: Ext4, Btrfs, XFS, ZFS and more
item.description: Linux supports quite a few filesystem types. Your choice of which filesystem to use can play an important role in optimizing performance, reliability and data integrity. Among the more popular filesystems are Ext4, Btrfs, XFS and ZFS. Each is widely used and offers some unique features and benefits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Inside Pinterest’s quest to restore internet optimism
item.description: Most social media platforms have devolved into a network of bottom-feeders and rage-baiters. Yet somehow, Pinterest has kept its joy. According to Sprout Social’s 2024 content strategy report, more than half of social users think of Pinterest as “more positive” than other platforms.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft Scraps Live Wallpapers for Windows 11 - A Bitter Blow for Fans
item.description: Microsoft has abandoned the concept of live wallpapers for Windows 11, leaving fans with a bitter disappointment.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US PC prices could soar if Trump tariff threats come true
item.description: Proposed import tariffs for the US could see PC costs rise as much as 46%, with smartphones also becoming more expensive.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: The ruling hands a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden admin officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Windows 11 24H2 is in danger of becoming hated by gamers
item.description: Windows 11’s 24H2 update has hit gamers with quite a number of bugs, and now Path of Exile 2 players are being made miserable.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Peer-to-Peer Car-Sharing Platform Turo Faces Scrutiny After Deadly Attacks
item.description: Two horrific attacks on New Year's Day, one in Las Vegas and the other in New Orleans, have raised serious questions about car-sharing platform security and potential terrorism links. The vehicles involved were rented through Turo.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Windsurf Cascade uses AI to help beginners code games and applications
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. I don’t know how to code.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft claims Edge had a great year, boasts soaring browser user numbers
item.description: Microsoft claims 10 billion GenAI chats happened from within Edge, but browser still lags behind Chrome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Health AI: How Apple can boost public health
item.description: Combined with its existing solutions, Apple’s strategic approach to artificial intelligence (AI) deployment could make a radical difference to public health. Here is how it could achieve that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US Government Must Keep Pace with AI-Empowered Citizenry
item.description: The US government must adapt to the rapidly evolving world of artificial intelligence (AI) to maintain trust and stay competitive in a global economy increasingly driven by AI-powered services.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Rhode Island’s state health benefits website hacked, user data exposed
item.description: Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: AI vaporware: 7 products that didn’t materialize in 2024
item.description: Whether to raise money, placate shareholders, or generate positive press, AI’s biggest companies have a habit of announcing advancements that are nowhere near ready to ship.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: US Treasury says it was breached by Chinese-backed hacker
item.description: The hacker was able to remotely access certain Treasury workstations and “certain unclassified documents maintained by those users,” the department said.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: The best new Windows 11 features of 2024
item.description: Microsoft is a little sneaky. Sure, there’s just one “big” update for Windows 11 each year. But Microsoft’s Windows team is always working on something, and new Windows 11 features are arriving on PCs every month — even outside of those high-profile updates.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The long-term impacts of AI on networking
item.description: AI is either going to spawn a golden age of profits and benefits or kill us all off, according to popular viewpoints on the subject. The latter view has been at least somewhat discredited; if you yell “AI” in a crowded theater these days, hardly anyone runs for the exits.
item.dc:creator: Network World
